notes: Works fine even in 4k with DXVK without any modification. Though the framerate could be a little better. On Plasma Wayland I had to set the game to window mode with boarders to see the output.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Playable, stutters here and there but not game-changing
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: The game runs fine and I haven't noticed any crashing or game wrecking glitches; however, the performance is not at all consistent. I've had fun with it, and I plan to keep playing, but every 30 or 40 seconds the framerate tanks and sometimes even hangs at 1 or 2 for a split second. This issue is not related to graphical demand because it seems to have no relation to the action on the screen. If I just stare at a wall I see the hiccuping too.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Runs fine at 60FPS with the odd frame rate dip here and there when there's a lot going on onscreen. No graphical anomalies observed. Audio works as expected. Didn't have to tweak anything to get it running. Easily comparable to Windows.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: No tweaks whatsoever needed. Ran straight out of the box at Ultra, some very slight stuttering but generally FPS's around 90-120.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes:
would not run under 4.2 but ran without any tweaks under 3.16 beta.

Locked up desktop once in 45 hours but otherwise has been perfectly stable. Stutters for a fraction of a second, every so many seconds, but not sure whether this is tied to proton as windows users seem to have similar complaints, but I find it quite playable.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Game runs flawlessly out of the box. 60FPS @1080p with settings on High and SMAA x2.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: The game always loads just at the first mission the tunnel and does not exit, having to kill the process.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Performance is fine overall with some small stutter during shader compilation. The problem is that there is some regular stutter (peak in frame times). That issue finally got much better by switching to borderless window and disabling vsync.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Has noticeable frame loss, even on lowest settings, but still playable. Has a big issue with crashing as well. When I disabled vsync, it seemed to increase frames, yet the noticeable frame loss is still there.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: works OOTB. some framedrops. if you use valves mesa with ACO you have to disable  ACO: RADV_PERFTEST=llvm %command%
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Works out of the box but with some framedrop
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Just click play. Graphic setting: very high. FPS 50-55.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes:
Still has noticeable frame loss, but is much more comfortable to play with from last report. I did experience a crash after at least an hour of game play. Now, the biggest issue is save points. Perhaps this is a game issue itself? I read up online that the save points are at weapons lockers. But I captured a ton of strong points from the KPA, and used a weapons locker each time. And I believe it is also supposed to save when you "wake up" after dying. Instead, once the game DID crash, it returned me to the starting point of the first mission after you defeat the convoy with the barrels. This is frustrating, however I don't know whether it's an issue with Proton-- or if it is an issue with the actual game.

Here is a similar/the same issue:
https://steamcommunity.com/app/223100/discussions/0/154645539344397564/
title: Homefront®: The Revolution
